title: Composition and delegation

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific C++: Beginner to Intermediate draft for Composition and delegation; human standards, technical, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: C++ Core Guidelines; living guidelines accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Composition and delegation
sources.claim: Composition builds behavior from owned or referenced components and is preferred when the relationship is has-a or implemented-in-terms-of rather than substitutable is-a.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Composition builds behavior from owned or referenced components and is preferred when the relationship is has-a or implemented-in-terms-of rather than substitutable is-a.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A Report owns a Formatter component and delegates formatting without claiming that a Report is a kind of Formatter. Your goal is to refactor an inheritance-shaped design into composition when substitution is not needed. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Class interface and invariants; unique_ptr, shared_ptr and non-owning access. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language rule and operational model
sections.body: Composition builds behavior from owned or referenced components and is preferred when the relationship is has-a or implemented-in-terms-of rather than substitutable is-a. This rule is applied to concrete C++ source and observable behavior, not treated as a vocabulary definition alone.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Composition still needs ownership and lifetime design, forwarding can enlarge interfaces, and runtime strategy replacement can require indirection. Therefore the draft explicitly rejects this beginner failure model: Inheritance is always the best reuse mechanism because composition requires writing forwarding methods.

includes: Build behavior from owned member objects with explicit responsibilities.
Refactor an inheritance-shaped design into composition when substitution is not needed.
excludes: Composition still needs ownership and lifetime design, forwarding can enlarge interfaces, and runtime strategy replacement can require indirection.

outcomes: Refactor an inheritance-shaped design into composition when substitution is not needed.

examples.title: Composition and delegation: defined case
examples.situation: A Report owns a Formatter component and delegates formatting without claiming that a Report is a kind of Formatter.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the concrete source or state: A Report owns a Formatter component and delegates formatting without claiming that a Report is a kind of Formatter.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the conclusion: Choose composition for has-a collaboration and inheritance only for a sound substitutable abstraction with an intentionally virtual interface.
examples.result: The defined result follows because Choose composition for has-a collaboration and inheritance only for a sound substitutable abstraction with an intentionally virtual interface.

examples.title: Composition and delegation: boundary case
examples.situation: A service receives a Clock interface component for testing rather than inheriting unrelated clock state.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Identify the changed source or state: A service receives a Clock interface component for testing rather than inheriting unrelated clock state.
Step 2 — Keep this standard boundary visible: Composition still needs ownership and lifetime design, forwarding can enlarge interfaces, and runtime strategy replacement can require indirection.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting rule: Inheritance is always the best reuse mechanism because composition requires writing forwarding methods.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Refactor an inheritance-shaped design into composition when substitution is not needed.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: Inheritance is always the best reuse mechanism because composition requires writing forwarding methods.
misconceptions.correction: Choose composition for has-a collaboration and inheritance only for a sound substitutable abstraction with an intentionally virtual interface.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Using concrete CPP rules, explain why this belief fails: Inheritance is always the best reuse mechanism because composition requires writing forwarding methods.

analogy.example: A system of self-managing components can model Composition and delegation: each component protects an invariant and owns resources through typed interfaces and lifetime.
usefulFor: Connecting types, value semantics, invariants, generic interfaces, ownership, and deterministic cleanup
limitations: C++ overload resolution, templates, value categories, object lifetime, and undefined behavior have no exact physical equivalent.
Composition still needs ownership and lifetime design, forwarding can enlarge interfaces, and runtime strategy replacement can require indirection.

recap.summary: You should now be able to refactor an inheritance-shaped design into composition when substitution is not needed. Keep this boundary in view: Composition still needs ownership and lifetime design, forwarding can enlarge interfaces, and runtime strategy replacement can require indirection.
